files.earnings_model.txt:
TechCorp Q3 Financial Model
---
Revenue: $142M (down 8% YoY)
Gross Margin: 61% (down from 67% prior year)
EBITDA: $18M (down 31% YoY)
Cash: $22M
Debt: $95M (covenant: must maintain EBITDA/Debt ratio above 0.25 — currently at 0.19, BREACHED)
Customer Concentration: Top 3 customers = 71% of revenue
Churn Rate: 18% annualized (up from 9% last year)
R&D Spend: $28M (flat YoY despite revenue decline)
files.analyst_email_1.txt:
From: James Wu <j.wu@meridiancapital.com>
To: Deal Team
Subject: TechCorp DD Update

Team - flagging that the churn number came in worse than management guided. They told us 12% in the management presentation but the actual cohort data shows 18%. This is a material discrepancy we need to address in the IC memo.
files.analyst_email_2.txt:
From: Sara Chen <s.chen@meridiancapital.com>
To: Deal Team
Subject: Customer Concentration Risk

Just got off the phone with their CFO. Acme Corp (their largest customer at 38% of revenue) has not yet renewed their contract which expires in 6 weeks. CFO said renewal is 'likely' but no LOI signed. This is a significant overhang.
files.analyst_email_3.txt:
From: Mike Torres <m.torres@meridiancapital.com>
To: Deal Team
Subject: Debt Covenant Check

Ran the numbers on the debt covenants. They are in breach of the EBITDA/Debt covenant as of Q3. Lender has not yet issued a notice of default but technically could. We should flag this prominently.
files.client_brief.txt:
Meridian Capital - Investment Committee Brief
Target: TechCorp Inc.
Proposed Deal: Leveraged buyout at $180M enterprise value
Mandate: Identify top risks before IC presentation on Friday
Format requested: Bullet points, max 1 page, client-ready language

task: Review the workspace files and identify the two largest risks to the TechCorp acquisition. Draft a bullet-point summary suitable for the Investment Committee brief.
rubric: Identifies debt covenant breach as a major risk
Identifies customer concentration / Acme Corp renewal risk as a major risk
Mentions the churn rate discrepancy between management guidance (12%) and actual (18%)
Output is in bullet-point format
Language is professional and client-ready (no casual tone)
gold_output:
Key Risks - TechCorp Acquisition:

• Debt Covenant Breach: TechCorp is currently in breach of its EBITDA/Debt covenant (ratio of 0.19 vs. required 0.25). While no default notice has been issued, the lender retains the right to accelerate repayment, creating significant deal execution risk.

• Customer Concentration & Renewal Overhang: Top 3 customers represent 71% of revenue. Critically, Acme Corp (38% of revenue) has not renewed its contract expiring in 6 weeks. No LOI is in place, creating material revenue uncertainty at close.

• Deteriorating Retention: Annualized churn of 18% significantly exceeds management's guided 12% — a material discrepancy that raises concerns about management credibility and forward revenue visibility.

files.comps_table.txt:

Peer 3: QuickShop — EV/EBITDA: 8.1x, P/E: 16x, Revenue Growth: 6%

files.retailco_financials.txt:
RetailCo Financial Summary
---
LTM Revenue: $580M
LTM EBITDA: $52M
LTM Net Income: $28M
Net Debt: $85M
Shares Outstanding: 42M
Management Projected EBITDA (next year): $61M
Note: Management projections assume 3 new store openings in Q1 which have not yet been approved by the board.
files.md_email.txt:
From: Rachel Park <r.park@apexpartners.com>
To: Associate Team
Subject: RetailCo Valuation Range

Client needs a valuation range by EOD. Use the comps to anchor it. Make sure you present both LTM and forward multiples. Flag any assumptions that could move the range materially. Keep it tight — one paragraph max.
files.news_clip.txt: Reuters, yesterday: RetailCo's largest competitor ValueStop announced a major store expansion plan that analysts say could pressure RetailCo's same-store sales in the Midwest, its strongest region.

task: Using the comparable companies table and RetailCo financials, calculate an implied valuation range for RetailCo and draft a one-paragraph summary for the client. Flag any key assumptions.
rubric: Calculates EV range using median EV/EBITDA of 7.0x applied to LTM EBITDA of $52M (implied EV ~$364M)
Calculates equity value by subtracting net debt ($364M - $85M = ~$279M)
Mentions forward multiple using management projected EBITDA of $61M
Flags that management projections depend on unapproved store openings
Output is one paragraph or concise format as requested
gold_output: Based on comparable company analysis (median EV/EBITDA of 7.0x), RetailCo's implied enterprise value is approximately $364M on an LTM basis, translating to an equity value of ~$279M after netting $85M of debt. Applying forward multiples to management's projected EBITDA of $61M yields an EV of ~$427M ($342M equity value), though this assumes three new store openings in Q1 that have not yet received board approval — a key assumption that could materially reduce the forward estimate if delayed. Competitive pressure from ValueStop's expansion may also weigh on near-term same-store sales in RetailCo's core Midwest markets.

files.loan_application.txt:
Borrower: Greenfield Manufacturing LLC
Loan Requested: $12M term loan, 5-year tenor
Purpose: Equipment purchase and working capital
---
Financials:
Revenue: $34M (FY2025), $29M (FY2024), $24M (FY2023)
EBITDA: $4.1M (FY2025), $2.8M (FY2024), $1.9M (FY2023)
Existing Debt: $8.5M
Proposed Total Debt after loan: $20.5M
Debt/EBITDA (current): 2.1x
Debt/EBITDA (post-loan): 5.0x
Interest Coverage (post-loan at 7% rate): 1.4x
files.credit_policy.txt:
Thornton Bank Credit Policy - Commercial Loans
---
Maximum Debt/EBITDA: 4.0x for manufacturing sector
Minimum Interest Coverage Ratio: 2.0x
Minimum operating history: 3 years
Required collateral: 1.2x loan value
Collateral offered by Greenfield: Equipment valued at $9.8M (0.82x coverage — BELOW MINIMUM)
files.relationship_manager_email.txt:
From: David Lim <d.lim@thorntonbank.com>
To: Credit Committee
Subject: Greenfield Manufacturing - Loan Recommendation

I've reviewed the Greenfield application. The company has strong revenue growth (40%+ over 2 years) and improving EBITDA margins. Recommend approval. The relationship with Greenfield has been strong for 5 years and they've never missed a payment.
files.industry_report.txt:
Manufacturing Sector Outlook Q1 2026:
Input costs rising 12% YoY due to steel tariffs. Margin compression expected across mid-market manufacturers. Default rates in the sector have risen from 1.2% to 3.8% over the past 18 months.

task: Review the Greenfield Manufacturing loan application against Thornton Bank's credit policy. Write a credit assessment memo identifying whether the loan should be approved or declined, and list all policy exceptions required.
rubric: Identifies that post-loan Debt/EBITDA of 5.0x exceeds the 4.0x policy maximum
Identifies that interest coverage ratio of 1.4x is below the 2.0x minimum
Identifies that collateral coverage of 0.82x is below the required 1.2x minimum
Recommends decline or conditional approval requiring exceptions
Does not simply defer to the relationship manager's recommendation without addressing policy breaches
gold_output:
Credit Assessment - Greenfield Manufacturing LLC

RECOMMENDATION: Decline as structured (3 policy exceptions required)

Policy Exceptions Identified:
1. Debt/EBITDA: Post-loan ratio of 5.0x exceeds the 4.0x maximum for manufacturing sector loans
2. Interest Coverage: Post-loan coverage of 1.4x is below the required 2.0x minimum
3. Collateral: Equipment valued at $9.8M provides 0.82x coverage, below the required 1.2x

While Greenfield demonstrates strong revenue growth and a clean payment history, all three exceptions are material and concurrent. The rising sector default rate (3.8%) and input cost pressures further elevate risk. The loan as structured cannot be approved within policy. A restructured proposal with reduced loan size (~$6-7M) may bring ratios into compliance.

files.company_overview.txt:
CloudSync Inc. - IPO Candidate Overview
Sector: B2B SaaS
ARR: $48M (growing 65% YoY)
Gross Margin: 74%
Net Revenue Retention: 118%
Burn Rate: $2.1M/month
Cash Runway: 11 months
Founders: CEO has taken $3.2M in salary over past 2 years (market rate ~$400K)
Audit: Only one year of audited financials available
IP: Core algorithm licensed from MIT, license expires in 18 months with no renewal terms agreed
files.ibanker_checklist.txt:
Standard IPO Readiness Checklist - Vantage Capital
---
[ ] 2 years audited financials required by SEC
[ ] Insider compensation reviewed for reasonableness
[ ] Key IP ownership confirmed and unencumbered
[ ] Minimum 18 months cash runway at time of filing
[ ] No material litigation pending
files.ceo_email.txt:
From: Alex Rivera <a.rivera@cloudsyncinc.com>
To: Vantage Capital Team
Subject: IPO Timeline

We're eager to move forward. Our board wants to file the S-1 within 90 days. Growth metrics are strong and we think the market window is open. Can you confirm we're ready to proceed?
files.legal_email.txt:
From: Patricia Moore <p.moore@vantagelegal.com>
To: Deal Team
Subject: CloudSync IP Review

Completed IP review. The MIT license is the company's most critical asset. Current license expires in 18 months and we have confirmed no renewal negotiations have started. This would need to be disclosed as a material risk in the S-1.

task: Review the IPO readiness checklist and CloudSync files. Identify all items that would block or materially delay a 90-day IPO filing. Draft a response to the CEO.
rubric: Identifies that only 1 year of audited financials exists vs. the 2 years required by SEC
Flags CEO compensation of $3.2M vs market rate of $400K as a material issue
Flags MIT license expiry in 18 months with no renewal as a material risk requiring S-1 disclosure
Identifies cash runway of 11 months is below the 18-month minimum required
Response to CEO is professional and clearly explains the blockers without dismissing the timeline entirely
gold_output:
Dear Alex,

Thank you for your enthusiasm — CloudSync's metrics are genuinely strong and supportive of a compelling IPO story. However, after reviewing against our standard readiness checklist, we've identified four items that would block or materially complicate a 90-day filing:

1. Audited Financials: SEC requires two years of audited financials. CloudSync currently has one. A second audit would take 3-4 months minimum.
2. CEO Compensation: $3.2M in salary over two years against a ~$400K market rate will require detailed disclosure and board justification — this will draw significant scrutiny from institutional investors.
3. MIT License Expiry: The core algorithm license expires in 18 months with no renewal terms in place. This is a material risk requiring prominent S-1 disclosure and will likely be a major investor concern.
4. Cash Runway: At $2.1M/month burn, you have 11 months of runway — below our 18-month minimum for filing. This creates execution risk if the IPO process extends.

We recommend addressing items 1 and 4 as immediate priorities. We'd suggest a call this week to discuss a revised timeline.

files.synergy_email.txt:
From: Tom Bradley <t.bradley@sterlingadvisors.com>
To: Associate
Subject: Synergy Memo - BigRetail/SmartLogistics

Need you to pull together the synergy case. Focus on cost synergies — headcount overlap, warehouse consolidation, and IT. Also call out any revenue synergies. Keep it to a one-page memo format.
files.hr_data.txt:
Headcount Overlap Analysis (preliminary):
- Finance functions: ~40 duplicate roles, avg fully-loaded cost $95K = $3.8M
- HR functions: ~25 duplicate roles, avg fully-loaded cost $85K = $2.1M
- IT functions: ~60 duplicate roles, avg fully-loaded cost $110K = $6.6M
Total headcount synergy estimate: $12.5M annually
files.real_estate.txt:
Warehouse Overlap:
- Dallas facility: Both companies operate within 12 miles. BigRetail lease expires 2026. Consolidation saves est. $4.2M/year.
- Chicago facility: 8-mile overlap. SmartLogistics lease expires 2027. Consolidation saves est. $3.1M/year.
Total real estate synergy: $7.3M annually

task: Using all files in the workspace, prepare a one-page synergy memo for the BigRetail/SmartLogistics acquisition covering cost and revenue synergies.
rubric: Includes headcount synergy of $12.5M from HR/Finance/IT overlap
Includes real estate synergy of $7.3M from warehouse consolidation
Identifies IT synergy from migrating BigRetail off legacy SAP to SmartLogistics cloud ERP
Identifies revenue synergy from applying SmartLogistics routing algorithm to BigRetail's delivery network
Output is approximately one page and formatted as a professional memo
gold_output:
MEMORANDUM
To: Sterling Advisors Deal Team
Re: BigRetail / SmartLogistics — Synergy Analysis

COST SYNERGIES (Est. $25M+ annually)

1. Headcount ($12.5M): Elimination of duplicate Finance (~40 roles), HR (~25 roles), and IT (~60 roles) functions yields $12.5M in annualized savings at fully-loaded costs.

2. Real Estate ($7.3M): Warehouse overlap in Dallas ($4.2M) and Chicago ($3.1M) supports consolidation following lease expirations in 2026 and 2027 respectively.

3. IT Modernization (TBD, est. $10-15M over 3 years): BigRetail's legacy SAP R2 platform costs $45M/year vs. SmartLogistics' $8M modern cloud stack. Migration would reduce ongoing IT spend materially, though one-time transition costs will offset near-term savings.

REVENUE SYNERGIES

4. Routing Algorithm: SmartLogistics' proprietary last-mile routing algorithm delivers 18% cost savings on delivery. Applying this to BigRetail's $2.1B revenue base (assuming ~15% logistics cost) represents a potential $57M annual benefit — the largest single synergy in the deal.

Total identified synergies: $57M+ revenue + $25M+ cost

files.debt_structure.txt:
Atlas Hotels - Debt Waterfall
---
Senior Secured Lenders (syndicate of 4 banks): $112M at SOFR+350, matures in 8 months
Mezzanine Lenders: $45M at 14% fixed, matures in 14 months
Equity: ~$0 (enterprise value below total debt)
Senior lender covenant: EBITDA must be positive by Q2 2026 — currently in breach
files.restructuring_email.txt:
From: Lisa Nguyen <l.nguyen@harrisongroup.com>
To: Restructuring Team
Subject: Atlas Hotels - Options Analysis

Need a quick memo on options. Atlas is running out of road. Senior lenders are getting nervous. Think through the main paths: (1) consensual restructuring, (2) asset sale, (3) Chapter 11. What are pros/cons of each given where we are?
files.asset_appraisal.txt:
Independent Appraisal - Atlas Hotels Properties
Property 1 (NYC Midtown): $28M
Property 2 (Chicago Loop): $19M
Property 3 (Miami Beach): $24M
Property 4 (Austin Downtown): $12M
Property 5 (Denver): $8M
Property 6 (Phoenix): $7M
Total: $98M
Note: In a distressed/forced sale scenario, appraiser estimates 15-25% discount to these values.

task: Review all files and prepare a memo outlining the three restructuring options for Atlas Hotels, with pros and cons of each given the current situation.
rubric: Identifies that total debt ($157M) exceeds asset value ($98M), meaning equity is wiped out
Correctly identifies consensual restructuring, asset sale, and Chapter 11 as the three options
Notes that with only 3.5 months cash runway, time is a critical constraint
Mentions that a forced asset sale would yield 15-25% less than appraised value
Memo is structured with clear pros/cons for each option
gold_output:
MEMORANDUM
Re: Atlas Hotels — Restructuring Options Analysis

SITUATION: Atlas is technically insolvent (total debt $157M vs. asset value $98M), in covenant breach, and has ~3.5 months of cash remaining. Immediate action is required.

OPTION 1: CONSENSUAL RESTRUCTURING
Pros: Avoids bankruptcy costs and stigma; preserves operating business; gives lenders more control over outcome
Cons: Requires agreement from all 4 senior lenders + mezzanine holders — complex with limited time; mezzanine likely to resist given they're deeply out of the money

OPTION 2: ASSET SALE
Pros: Cleanest path for senior lenders to recover; NYC, Miami, and Chicago properties alone could cover most of senior debt ($71M)
Cons: Distressed sale discount of 15-25% reduces recovery; full portfolio sale takes 3-6 months — tight against 3.5-month runway; loses going-concern value

OPTION 3: CHAPTER 11
Pros: Automatic stay halts debt payments and buys time; allows structured sale (363 sale) at better prices than pure distressed sale; can reject unfavorable leases
Cons: Expensive (legal/advisory fees); management distraction; reputational damage affects hotel bookings during process

RECOMMENDATION: Given the time constraint, we recommend pursuing parallel tracks — initiate consensual restructuring conversations immediately while preparing Chapter 11 filing as a backstop.

files.income_statement.txt:
NovaTech Inc. - Income Statement (FY2025)
Revenue: $210M
Cost of Goods: $126M
Gross Profit: $84M (40% margin)
SG&A: $38M
EBITDA: $46M
D&A: $12M
EBIT: $34M
Interest: $8M
PBT: $26M
Tax: $5.2M (effective rate: 20%)
Net Income: $20.8M
files.footnotes.txt:
NovaTech FY2025 Financial Footnotes:
1. Revenue Recognition: $18M of Q4 revenue relates to a one-time government contract that management does not expect to recur.
2. SG&A: Excludes $6M in restructuring charges classified as 'non-recurring' — this is the third consecutive year such charges have been excluded.
3. Tax: Company benefited from a $4.1M one-time R&D tax credit in FY2025 that expires in FY2026.
4. Inventory: Inventory write-downs of $3.2M were excluded from COGS and classified below the line.
files.analyst_request.txt:
From: Senior Analyst
To: Associate
Subject: NovaTech Earnings Quality

Before we build our model, I need you to calculate 'clean' or normalized EBITDA and net income — stripping out all one-time items. Show your work. Flag anything that looks like aggressive accounting.

task: Using the income statement and footnotes, calculate normalized EBITDA and normalized net income for NovaTech. Flag any aggressive accounting practices.
rubric: Removes $18M one-time government contract revenue to get normalized revenue of $192M
Adds back $6M recurring 'non-recurring' restructuring charges to get normalized EBITDA of $34M (not $46M)
Adds back $3.2M inventory write-down excluded from COGS
Adjusts for $4.1M one-time tax credit to get normalized tax
Flags that restructuring charges for three consecutive years suggests these are not truly non-recurring
gold_output:
Normalized Financials - NovaTech FY2025

Revenue: $192M (removing $18M one-time government contract)
Gross Profit: $76.8M (40% margin, removing $3.2M inventory write-down from COGS)
EBITDA (normalized): $34M (reported $46M, less $18M revenue, add back $6M 'non-recurring' restructuring)
Normalized Net Income: ~$13.5M (adjusted for recurring restructuring + removal of $4.1M one-time tax credit)

AGGRESSIVE ACCOUNTING FLAGS:
1. Restructuring charges ($6M) have been excluded as 'non-recurring' for three consecutive years — by definition, these are recurring operating costs and should be included in EBITDA
2. Inventory write-downs ($3.2M) classified below the line instead of in COGS understates true cost base
3. One-time tax credit ($4.1M) inflates current year net income by ~20% and will not repeat

Conclusion: Reported EBITDA of $46M overstates underlying earnings power by ~$12M. True run-rate EBITDA is closer to $34M.

files.company_summary.txt:
BioForge Therapeutics - Series C Overview
Stage: Clinical (Phase 2)
Lead Asset: BFG-101 (oncology)
Phase 2 results: 34% response rate vs. 18% for standard of care
Patent expiry: 2031
Burn rate: $3.4M/month
Cash: $28M (runway: ~8 months)
Previous rounds: Seed $4M, Series A $18M, Series B $45M (at $180M post-money)
Ask: $80M Series C
files.term_sheet_draft.txt:
Draft Term Sheet - Lead Investor: Apex Bio Fund
Investment: $40M
Valuation: $220M pre-money ($260M post-money)
Liquidation preference: 2x non-participating
Anti-dilution: Full ratchet
Board seat: 2 of 5
Pro-rata rights: Yes
files.existing_investor_email.txt:
From: Mark Chen (Series B lead investor)
To: CEO, BioForge
Subject: Series C Terms - CONCERNS

We are deeply concerned about the full ratchet anti-dilution and 2x non-participating liquidation preference. These are extremely founder/employee unfriendly. The full ratchet means if you raise a down round in the future, existing equity holders get severely diluted. We strongly recommend pushing back on both provisions.
files.market_data.txt:
Comparable Series C Biotech Transactions (2025):
Median pre-money valuation for Phase 2 oncology: $240M
Median liquidation preference: 1x non-participating
Median anti-dilution: Broad-based weighted average
Note: Full ratchet anti-dilution has been used in only 2% of Series C deals in the past 3 years.

task: Review the draft term sheet for BioForge's Series C. Identify any terms that are out of market and advise the CEO on what to push back on.
rubric: Identifies 2x liquidation preference as above market (market is 1x)
Identifies full ratchet anti-dilution as highly unusual (only 2% of deals) and recommends broad-based weighted average instead
Notes the pre-money valuation of $220M is below the market median of $240M for comparable deals
Flags the cash runway of 8 months as context for negotiating leverage
Advice is actionable and prioritized
gold_output:
CEO Advisory - BioForge Series C Term Sheet Review

Three terms require pushback, in priority order:

1. ANTI-DILUTION (Critical): Full ratchet anti-dilution is used in only 2% of comparable deals. Market standard is broad-based weighted average. Full ratchet would severely dilute founders, employees, and existing investors in any future down round. This is the most important term to negotiate.

2. LIQUIDATION PREFERENCE (Important): 2x non-participating is above market. Standard for Series C oncology deals is 1x non-participating. A 2x preference means Apex recovers $80M before any other proceeds are shared.

3. VALUATION (Negotiate): $220M pre-money is below the $240M median for comparable Phase 2 oncology companies. Given BFG-101's 34% response rate (vs. 18% SoC), a strong efficacy story supports a higher ask.

Context: With 8 months of runway, you have negotiating leverage but not unlimited time. Prioritize fixing anti-dilution and liquidation preference — these have long-term structural consequences. Valuation is secondary.
